title: The “Historical Materials BAG”: A New Facilitated Access to Synchrotron X-ray Diffraction Analyses for Cultural Heritage Materials at the European Synchrotron Radiation Facility
type1_summary: No figure-ready or cleaned dataset files were identified.
type2_summary: No raw/unprocessed experimental data files were identified.
classification_notes: Paper evidence indicates no explicit data availability statement and no downloadable data files were identified. The only inspected file is the manuscript PDF, so there is no dataset to classify as Type 1 or Type 2.

file_classifications.paper_evidence: The paper describes a synchrotron X-ray powder diffraction study and the provided file is the paper PDF itself, not a data deposit. The paper analysis reports no data availability statement and no downloadable data files or URLs were identified on the publisher page.
file_classifications.file_evidence: PDF, 3.7 MB, sourced from Zenodo. This is consistent with an article/manuscript document rather than a dataset file. No tabular structure, figure-specific data organization, or instrument output is indicated from the file report.
file_classifications.reasoning: This file is the publication PDF, so it is documentation rather than a dataset. It does not contain replot-ready data or raw measurements as a standalone data file.

paper_analysis.summary: The paper describes a new ESRF block allocation access mode for synchrotron X-ray powder diffraction studies of cultural heritage materials and reports initial applications to pigments, paintings, ceramics, and wood.
paper_analysis.processed_plot_details: The paper appears to describe a beamtime/access mode and initial application examples for cultural heritage materials, but no explicit dataset or source data location is provided in the available information.
